key_insight: The paper introduces a stochastic elastoplastic contact model for rough surfaces that incorporates scale-dependent hardness, capturing the transition from elastic to plastic contact across surface scales.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a more realistic way to predict contact area, pressure, and yield behavior in rough interfaces relevant to tribology, electrical contacts, and surface design.
method: Formulates compounded Chapman–Kolmogorov integral equations for evolving probability densities across geometrical scales.
Tracks three contact-state distributions: elastic pressure, relative plastic area, and relative non-contact area.
Derives a new topographic yield parameter and a rapid contact-status diagram for classification.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical solutions and curve-fitting used to identify elastic and plastic limits and to verify smooth transitions from elasticity to full plasticity.
watch_item: The model’s accuracy and practicality will depend on how well the assumed stochastic roughness and scale-dependent hardness parameters match a given real surface.
paper_title: A stochastic model for elastoplastic contact of rough surfaces incorporating scale-dependent hardness
